_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 at 100, stands at 141, indicating a 41% rise since that base year. This increase reflects recent and uneven price changes across the components rather than a steady climb. The index captures global commodity prices, highlighting variability rather than a uniform inflation across all items. The rise is notable but not driven by general inflation trends in broader markets.
notes.movers: The primary contributors to the basket’s rise are coffee, which increased by 99%, and orange juice, up 34% since 2015. Wheat and sugar showed more moderate increases of 19% and 13%, respectively. These changes stem largely from weather conditions and crop diseases, such as frost and drought impacting arabica coffee regions and citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice production. The soft commodities involved have relatively thin markets, making prices more sensitive to supply shocks from these factors rather than broad economic inflation.
notes.why: The headline increase in the basket is driven by a few weather-exposed crops vulnerable to environmental shocks. Because these are narrow, specialized markets, supply disruptions can lead to sustained periods of elevated prices. This dynamic suggests that prices for these inputs can remain high over an extended timeframe without widespread inflationary pressures in other commodity sectors.
